candidates.name: Chris Yakabouski
candidates.notes: Longest-serving BOS member (since 2003). Self-identifies as far from moderate. Won 2025 re-election by only 16 votes vs. Braswell. Has clashed with local tea party/Patriots group over stadium and taxes. Runs Bob Yak and Sons Home Remodeling. Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism. Pragmatic Republican — not a culture warrior.

profile.background: Conservative Spotsylvania supervisor, Battlefield District

claims.text: Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism.

candidates.name: David Goosman
candidates.notes: STRONGEST CHRISTIAN CONSERVATIVE on the board. Baptist deacon who explicitly campaigns on Christian conservative principles. Retired Air Force officer, 21 years military service. Campaign website is goosmangop.org — the 'GOP' is in the URL. Defeated incumbent Kevin Marshall by a wide margin running as the more conservative option. His organization is explicitly 'committed to advancing Christian conservative leadership in Spotsylvania County.' Newly elected — limited voting record but strong faith-based platform.

candidates.name: Gerald Childress
candidates.notes: Marine veteran and Spotsylvania County native. Graduated from Spotsylvania High School. Served on Planning Commission (Chairman), Transportation Committee, and Economic Development Authority before BOS. Explicitly states belief in 'limited government, individual responsibility, and fiscal conservatism.' Initially pro-data-center development but shifted to support SUP requirement. First term — building voting record.

candidates.name: Drew Mullins
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Courtland District (Vice Chairman)
candidates.notes: Most decorated military veteran on the board — 23-year Navy SEAL with 14+ deployments. Elected Vice Chairman in Jan 2026 (Frazier was perturbed by the vote). Campaign focuses on economic development, competitive county employee salaries, and public safety — NOT culture war issues. Pragmatic Republican who emphasizes 'healthy county growth.' Small business owner (Azimuth Analytics). UMW graduate pursuing graduate degree at University of Arizona.

candidates.name: Lori Hayes
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)
candidates.notes: Board Chairman and independent who caucuses with Republicans. Social work degree from GMU. Co-owned Calypso Kennels in Stafford with her brother (who died in 2023). Strongest 2A score due to proactive leadership on sanctuary reaffirmation. Runs as independent — NOT endorsed through GOP nomination. Supported redistricting referendum opposition (6-0). Pragmatic, growth-management focused. Limited public positions on social/cultural issues — scores lower on life, marriage, and christian_nationalism due to absence of evidence, not contrary evidence.

profile.background: Chairman, Spotsylvania Board of Supervisors, Lee Hill District

candidates.name: Jacob Lane
candidates.notes: Blue-collar Republican — Luck Stone foreman who has been involved in county issues since his 20s. Won Feb 2022 special election after Barry Jett's death. Claims ideological alignment with Jett. Vocally defended the board's redistricting referendum resolution saying it was about 'equal and fair' timing for both sides. One of the most vocal 2A supporters — brought it up proactively during the Jan 2026 meeting. Youngest BOS member at ~40. Infrastructure and growth management focused.

candidates.name: Dr. Deborah H. Frazier
candidates.notes: LONE DEMOCRAT on the board. First African American BOS member in Spotsylvania history. Career educator (25-year principal, now Chief Academic Officer). Abstained from collective bargaining opposition letter (6-0, she abstained) saying she was 'on the fence.' Abstained from redistricting referendum opposition (6-0, she abstained). Was 'perturbed' when Mullins was elected Vice Chair over her or her preferred candidate in Jan 2026. Served on Spanberger's gubernatorial transition team. Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county. Scored 2 on self_defense because she did support 2A sanctuary. Education score reflects career dedication but from progressive establishment perspective, not parental rights/school choice angle.

claims.text: Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county.

candidates.name: Kerry Devine
candidates.notes: Mayor since 2024. Registered Independent but Dem-endorsed. Council under her leadership: LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, DEI commitment, 100% renewable energy resolution. No public stances on abortion, marriage, immigration, or religious liberty. Supports gun giveback program.

profile.education: University of Mary Washington
profile.background: Social studies teacher, Stafford County. City Council since 2004. School Board 1996-2004. Mother of four.

candidates.name: Charlie Frye Jr.
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive on council. Teamster (Local 322), 24-yr UPS driver. Created gun giveback program (200+ guns collected since 2014). Led slave auction block removal. 100% renewable energy by 2035. Progressive Voters Guide "most progressive choice." Member Shiloh Baptist Church but policy positions entirely progressive.

profile.background: Vice Mayor since 2020. Ward 4 Councilman since 2014. Only Black member of council. Gun give-back program. Racial equity advocate.

candidates.name: Jonathan Gerlach
candidates.notes: Attorney and retired archaeologist. NAACP executive board member and Legal Redress Committee. Focuses on workforce development, historic preservation. Avoids single-issue politics. Won Ward 2 with 60%. Almost no culture-war positions documented. Resigned/replaced 2025; seat now held by Joy Y. Crump.

profile.background: Attorney (Gerlach Law Firm PLC). Retired archaeologist. Ward 2 representative.

candidates.name: Jason Graham
candidates.notes: Former Ward 1 rep (term ended Dec 2025). Progressive Voters Guide listed. Voted with council on renewable energy and equity initiatives. No culture-war positions documented.

profile.education: University of Missouri (Journalism), University of Rochester (MBA)
profile.background: Consultant, former Ward 1 councilman. Did not seek re-election.

candidates.name: Joshua Cole
candidates.notes: Supports enshrining reproductive freedom in Virginia Constitution | Progressive on LGBTQ+ issues — expected to vote for marriage equality amendment | Supports gun violence prevention measures | VCDL 0-1% (2024-25). Expected Family Foundation ~0%.

profile.background: Baptist pastor. First Black person elected from Fredericksburg region (2019, age 29). Lost 2021, won again 2023/2025.
profile.education: Liberty University (attended 3 years)

candidates.name: Libby Humphries
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive official in Fredericksburg. Cashless bail policy. Attended BLM protests. Supports Fair and Just Prosecution (progressive prosecutors org). Created Therapeutic Docket for mentally ill defendants. Supports felon voting rights restoration. NAACP Education Committee co-chair. Began career as public defender. Member St. George's Episcopal Church. Successfully prosecuted high school shooting (murder convictions) and child abuse (6 life sentences) — not soft on violent crime despite progressive philosophy.

profile.background: Career prosecutor since 1999. Served in Charlottesville (1999-2018) then Fredericksburg (2018-present). Criminal Justice Reform Alliance member.

candidates.name: Ryan Mehaffey
candidates.notes: Marine veteran who enlisted at 17 after 9/11. Exceptional academic record (summa cum laude BBA + JD/MBA in 3 years). 10+ year prosecution track record described as 'tough but fair.' Republican endorsed, ran unopposed. Condemned misleading sample ballots that confused voters — shows election integrity concern. Prosecution philosophy balances public safety with rehabilitation. Victim-focused approach. His office ran a public education program on gun laws — 'arming citizens with knowledge.' Self-identifies as 'Conservative' on campaign website.

profile.background: U.S. Marine veteran (enlisted 2002 after 9/11). Prosecutor with 10+ years trial experience. Father of four.

candidates.name: Eric Olsen
candidates.notes: Strong law-and-order prosecutor with impressive record. Republican but focused on prosecution rather than policy advocacy. Gun violence concern is notable but he addresses it through prosecution, not gun control legislation. Education category largely N/A for this office. Longest-serving current Stafford elected official in this scorecard.

profile.background: 30+ year Stafford prosecutor. VA Prosecutor of the Year 2008. Tried 1,100+ DV cases. Testified before US Senate Judiciary Committee 2017.

candidates.name: Winsome Earle-Sears
candidates.notes: Lt. Governor of Virginia (2022-2026). First Black woman elected statewide in VA. Born in Jamaica. Marine veteran. Conservative but lost 2025 gubernatorial race to Spanberger. Pro-life, pro-2A, pro-border security. Not US-born (Jamaica).

profile.education: Old Dominion University (BA), Regent University (MA)
profile.background: U.S. Marine veteran, small business owner, Lt. Governor of Virginia 2022-2026, first Black woman elected statewide in VA
profile.prev_election_opponent: Hala Ayala (D) in 2021 Lt Gov race

candidates.name: Tinesha Allen
candidates.notes: Limited public positions on national culture-war issues. Focus is on local governance — school funding, infrastructure, competitive pay. Absence from CRT vote is a significant data point. Governs in coalition with Diggs. Score of 1 in each category reflects Democratic affiliation with no contradicting conservative positions found, but also minimal direct evidence of actively opposing Christian conservative positions at local level.

candidates.name: Maya Guy
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors, Aquia District (Vice Chair)
candidates.notes: Most evidence comes from school board tenure. Actively pushed equity/DEI agenda in schools — diversity hiring, equity-based admissions changes, equity PD for staff. This record directly conflicts with Christian conservative education priorities. Newly seated on BOS (Jan 2026) so limited supervisor voting record.

profile.background: Former Vice Chairwoman of Stafford School Board. Nearly two decades of community advocacy.

candidates.name: Kecia Evans
candidates.notes: Most documented progressive positions of any BOS member due to 2021 delegate race. Moms Demand Action endorsement is explicit anti-2A marker. Explicit LGBTQ+ rights support. Criminal justice reform focus (reduce mass incarceration). Interesting: Regent University law degree is from Pat Robertson's school, but her positions do not reflect that institution's conservative values.

profile.education: BA Criminal Justice, MS Cybersecurity/Digital Forensics, LLM National Security Law
profile.background: 22+ years criminal justice. 18 years supporting public defenders. Former Chair Stafford Board of Zoning Appeals. NAACP Criminal Justice committee. Stafford resident since 2015.

candidates.name: Pamela Yeung
candidates.notes: More establishment Democrat than progressive firebrand. Focus is on fiscal management (public-private partnerships, revenue diversification) and education infrastructure. Less overtly progressive than Evans or Guy on social issues, but solidly aligned with Democratic caucus on all major votes. Baylor education is notable but does not appear to influence governance.
